items.q: Which type of nuclear radiation is most ionising?
items.correct: Alpha radiation — large, doubly charged particles cause the most ionisation per unit distance.
items.misconception: Gamma is most ionising because it can travel furthest and pass through almost anything.
items.random_wrong: Beta radiation is most ionising because it consists of fast electrons.
items.why: Students conflate penetrating power with ionising power — gamma penetrates most because it ionises least, the opposite of intuition.

items.q: For a projectile launched horizontally, what affects the horizontal component of velocity during flight (ignoring air resistance)?
items.correct: Nothing — horizontal velocity stays constant; only vertical velocity changes due to gravity.
items.misconception: Gravity slows down both the vertical and horizontal motion.
items.random_wrong: The horizontal velocity increases over time because the projectile is falling.
items.why: Students treat 'gravity affects all motion' too broadly, missing that gravity acts only vertically and the components are independent.

items.q: In which direction does an electric field point near a positive charge?
items.correct: Radially outwards — the field is defined by the force on a positive test charge, which is repelled.
items.misconception: Radially inwards because positive attracts negative.
items.random_wrong: There is no field around a positive charge unless another charge is present.
items.why: Students confuse the convention (positive test charge) and forget that an isolated positive source still has a field even without another charge.

items.q: Which best describes the second law of thermodynamics?
items.correct: The total entropy of an isolated system never decreases.
items.misconception: Energy is conserved in any process.
items.random_wrong: Heat flows spontaneously from cold to hot.
items.why: Correct answer: The total entropy of an isolated system never decreases.. “Energy is conserved in any process.” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: A heat engine operates between 500 K and 300 K. Its maximum theoretical efficiency is:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 40%. “20%” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
